description.value: The target's form appears blurry. It becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Concealed]. As the nature of this effect still leaves the target's location obvious, the target can't use this concealment to Hide or Sneak.

You launch a torrent of magical nanites that begin dissolving your target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, you deal 3d8 acid damage plus 1d6 persistent acid damage. On a critical hit, double the initial damage, but not the persistent damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

You unravel the magic behind a spell or effect. Attempt a counteract check against the target. If you successfully counteract a magic item, the item becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. This doesn't change the item's non-magical properties. If the target is an artifact or similar item, you automatically fail.

target.value: 1 spell effect or unattended magic item

description.value:
A shield of elemental energy protects a creature against one type of energy damage. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. The target and its gear gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Spell Effect: Resist Energy]

Heightened (4th) The resistance increases to 10, and you can target up to two creatures.

Heightened (7th) The resistance increases to 15, and you can target up to five creatures.

You ritually sift through the local infosphere and gain mystical insight about a topic. During the casting of this spell, ask about the results of a particular course of action. The spell can predict results up to 30 minutes into the future and reveals the GM's best guess among the following outcomes.

  • Weal The results will be good.
  • Woe The results will be bad.
  • Weal and Woe The results will be a mix of good and bad.
  • Nothing There won't be particularly good or bad results. The GM rolls a secret @Check[flat|dc:6|traits:secret]. On a failure, the result is always "nothing." This makes it impossible to tell whether a "nothing" result is accurate. If anyone asks about the same topic as the first casting of sift the sphere during an additional casting, the GM uses the secret roll result from the first casting. If circumstances change, it's possible to get a different result.

Heightened (4th) The spell can predict results up to 1 week into the future.

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Impersonate] the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Treat Wounds] on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Recall Knowledge] about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Spell Effect: Analyze Target]

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You sap the target's strength, depending on its Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 3} for 1 minute.

You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Fleeing] for 1 round.

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to five creatures.

You fire a shard of solidified magic toward a creature that you can see. It automatically hits and deals 1d4+1 force damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, increase the number of shards you shoot by one, to a maximum of three shards for 3 actions. You choose the target for each shard individually. If you shoot more than one shard at the same target, combine the damage before applying bonuses or penalties to damage, resistances, weaknesses, and so forth.

Heightened (+2) You fire one additional shard with each action you spend.

You manifest an injury from the future or past to harm a creature in the present. Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. The target takes 1d8 of the selected damage type with a basic Will save. If the target fails its save and then takes the same type of damage before the end of your next turn, it takes an additional @Damage[ceil((@item.level)/2)d4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a failure increases by 1d4.
You link your mind to the target's mind and mentally impart to that target an amount of information in an instant that could otherwise be communicated in 10 minutes.

The next time you make an attack roll before the end of your turn, roll it twice and use the better result. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any flat check required due to the target being @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Concealed] or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Hidden]. You are then temporarily immune to sure strike for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Spell Effect: Sure Strike]

duration.value: until the end of your turn

description.value:
You hurl a loose, unattended object that is within range and that has 1 Bulk or less at the target. Make a spell attack roll against the target. If you hit, you deal 2d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage—as appropriate for the object you hurled. No specific traits or magic properties of the hurled item affect the attack or the damage.

Critical Success You deal double damage.

Success You deal full damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

Trigger You fail an attack roll or saving throw.

You open your mind to read the underlying probability of the multiverse. Reroll the failed check and take the better result.

Upgrades: 1

Zero pistols use transparent canisters of supercooled liquid as ammunition.

Upgrades: 1

This form-fitting suit of carbon fiber encases the body. Most are usually dull-gray, functional garments, while pricier, bespoke suits dazzle like diamonds.

A parallel accord is a small metallic disk ringed with magical runes that transcend reality, serving as a nexus for a number of your parallel selves. When invested, a parallel accord hovers over your head like a crown.

Activate—Parallel Positioning r (concentration, teleportation)

Trigger You succeed at a saving throw against a spell or a Strike misses you

Frequency once per day

Effect You tap into one of the alternate realities linked to your parallel accord, repositioning yourself into a location you occupy in another reality. You teleport to any unoccupied square you can see within 20 feet. If you critically succeeded at the triggering saving throw or were critically missed by the triggering Strike, you additionally become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Concealed] until the beginning of your next turn, as your body is shrouded in fragmented reflections of other realities.

publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-16: The Beasts of Bo: Part Two

description.value:
Trigger Quantum Nexus rolls initiative

Effect Quantum Nexus releases a burst of energy and uses Warp Reality.

Trigger Quantum Nexus casts a 2nd-rank spell, and the spell targets a creature or area within her quantum field

Effect Quantum Nexus manipulates her quantum field to preserve some spell energy and regains an expended 1st-rank spell slot.

Frequency once per round

Effect Quantum Nexus transports herself in the above of an eye, teleporting to a location up to 20 feet away within her line of sight.

Requirements Quantum Nexus's quantum field isn't active

Effect Quantum Nexus's quantum field activates and lasts as long as she Sustains it (up to 10 minutes) or until the end of her next turn. The quantum field is a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] centered on a point she chooses within 100 feet. Whenever she Sustains it, she can [[/act seek]] or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Recall Knowledge] against a target in the field as a free action. If she Sustained it with the Sustain action (instead of an anchoring action), she can move her quantum field to a new position within 100 feet. The field automatically deactivates if she's knocked out, if she Dismisses it, or if she moves more than 100 feet away from it.

publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-16: The Beasts of Bo: Part Two
